visible_c_inputs.question: Who was the 2nd year head coach of the team who played against Florida State in the 101st Rose Bowl?
visible_c_inputs.summary_1:
First-hop evidence: Passage "2015 Rose Bowl" — "matched the Oregon Ducks against the Florida State Seminoles" (101st Rose Bowl).  
Bridge needed: Confirm who was the Oregon Ducks' head coach for the season culminating in the 101st Rose Bowl and whether that coach was in his 2nd year (candidate: Mark Helfrich).  
Retrieval direction: 1. Fetch the page "2014 Oregon Ducks football team" and inspect the Infobox/Head coach line and season summary. 2. Fetch the page "Mark Helfrich" and check his coaching tenure at Oregon (years/Head coaching record) to verify 2nd-year status.

current_output.query:
Fetch:
1) "2014 Oregon Ducks football team" — check Infobox and season summary for head coach and year.
2) "Mark Helfrich" — check coaching tenure and head coaching years to confirm 2014 was his 2nd year.

output.query:
Fetch:
1) "2014 Oregon Ducks football team" — check Infobox and season summary for head coach and year.
2) "Mark Helfrich" — check coaching tenure and head coaching years to confirm 2014 was his 2nd year.

retrieved_titles: List of Oregon Ducks head football coaches
retrieved_doc_snippets: 2014 Oregon Ducks football team | The 2014 Oregon Ducks football team represented the University of Oregon in the 2014 NCAA Division I FBS football season. The team was led by second-year head coach Mark Helfrich and played their home games at Autzen Stadium for the 48th straight year. They were a member of the Pac-12 Conference in the North Division.

2016 Oregon Ducks football team | The 2016 Oregon Ducks football team represented the University of Oregon in the 2016 NCAA Division I FBS football season. The team was led by fourth-year head coach Mark Helfrich and played their home games at Autzen Stadium for the 50th straight year. They were a member of the Pac-12 Conference in the North Division.

2013 Oregon Ducks football team | The 2013 Oregon Ducks football team represented the University of Oregon in the 2013 NCAA Division I FBS football season. The team was led by first year head coach Mark Helfrich and played their home games at Autzen Stadium for the 47th consecutive year. They were a member of the Pac-12 Conference in the North Division.

2015 Oregon Ducks football team | The 2015 Oregon Ducks football team represented the University of Oregon in the 2015 NCAA Division I FBS football season. The team was led by third-year head coach Mark Helfrich and played their home games at Autzen Stadium for the 49th straight year. They were a member of the Pac-12 Conference in the North Division.

2009 Oregon Ducks football team | The 2009 Oregon Ducks football team represented the University of Oregon in the 2009 NCAA Division I FBS football season. The team was led by head coach Chip Kelly in his first season as a head coach at the Division I FBS level. Kelly was only the third Ducks head coach since 1977 and led the Ducks to a Pac-10 Championship and was named Pac-10 Coach of the Year. He took over for Mike Bellotti.

List of Oregon Ducks head football coaches | The Oregon Ducks football program is a college football team representing the University of Oregon that is a member of the Pac-12 Conference. The team has had 32 head coaches since its founding in 1894. The Ducks have played in more than 1,100 games in 113 seasons. In those seasons, eight different coaches have led Oregon to bowl games: Hugo Bezdek, Shy Huntington, Jim Aiken, Len Casanova, Rich Brooks, Mike Bellotti, Chip Kelly and Mark Helfrich. 1996 Oregon Ducks football team | The 1996 Oregon Ducks football team represented the University of Oregon during the 1996 NCAA Division I-A football season. They were led by head coach Mike Bellotti, who was in his 2nd season as head coach of the Ducks. They played their home games at Autzen Stadium in Eugene, Oregon and participated as members of the Pacific-10 Conference.

Wikidumper.org | WikiDumper.org is a website created by Clifford A. Pickover that promises to permanently record a snapshot of the "best of the Wikipedia rejects", articles that are slated for deletion at the site. WikiDumper was launched on November 4, 2006, and accepts user submissions. Although the site doesn't specify its criteria for inclusion, many of its articles don't cite their sources. The site has been criticized as likely to be less accurate than Wikipedia.

delta_p.trigger: Apply when summary_1 names a specific page title to verify (e.g., '10 Downing Street') and the agent role is to produce a single second-hop retrieval query to confirm a concise common-name claim.
delta_p.preserve_rule: Keep using the provided `question` and `summary_1` as input; preserve inclusion of the named page title tokens from summary_1 as the focal retrieval target.
delta_p.change_rule: Instead of emitting multiple site-filtered or separate-line queries, produce exactly one compact, single-line natural-language query. Begin with the canonical page title token(s) taken from summary_1, then append a short clarifying phrase that asks for the specific fact to verify (for example, a brief phrase asking what the location is commonly called in the United Kingdom). Do not include retrieval instructions, multiple queries, 'site:' filters, or additional commentary.
delta_p.drop_or_avoid_rule: Do not output multiple queries or separate lines (no parallel '10 Downing Street' and 'Number 10' queries). Avoid adding site: filters, search operators, extra context, or the answer itself. Do not include meta-comments or instructions to the retriever.
delta_p.output_contract: Return a single-line string as the `query`. The line must start with the page title tokens from summary_1 and end with a concise natural-language verification phrase about the common UK name. No other text, no explanation, and no answer.
delta_p.why_local_transition: Combining the named page title with a short verification phrase focuses the retriever on the canonical page and the specific claim (common UK name), preventing noisy results caused by multiple separate or site-filtered queries.

match.transported_damage_hypothesis: The agent will replace the concise two-step fetch plan with multiple separate/site-filtered query lines (e.g., '2014 Oregon Ducks football team site:wikipedia.org' and 'Mark Helfrich site:wikipedia.org' or parallel queries), rather than emitting a single compact verification query that begins with the canonical page title and a short verification phrase.
match.expected_damaged_behavior: Retriever receives multiple fragmented/site-filtered queries and returns noisy, noncanonical results or misses the precise Mark Helfrich page; the agent fails to obtain the clear second-hop evidence that 2014 was Helfrich's 2nd year.
match.rationale: The C case and W sample share the same structural need: summary_1 names a specific canonical page that must be verified by a single focused second-hop retrieval. The W failure pattern concerns query shape—emitting multiple separate/site-filtered queries (and/or parallel lines) that preserve title tokens but diffuse the retriever's focus and produce noisy results. That exact failure (wrong query shape while still anchoring on the named page) can plausibly occur at the C agent boundary (the agent tasked to request the '2014 Oregon Ducks football team' and 'Mark Helfrich' evidence). Transporting this pattern keeps the anchor tokens and bridge intent but breaks the compact verification query the retriever needs, producing a local neighbor failure that leaves C_plus missing the second-hop evidence without requiring answer flips or new entities.

request.rendered_prompt:
You are constructing a target-specific transported damaged output C_minus
for one agent in a fixed HotpotQA multi-agent pipeline.

Inputs:
- C_plus is a correct current output/state for the target sample.
- W_minus -> W_plus is a matched W-side repair transition.
- The ordered W delta-p trace describes the repair behavior.

Task:
Infer the reverse W failure pattern and apply only that failure pattern to
the target sample's own visible inputs, producing a plausible LOCAL neighbor
C_minus of C_plus.

Strict rules:
- Produce an actual output for the selected agent boundary, not a prompt.
- Preserve the agent's native input/output contract.
- Use only visible_c_inputs as factual content.
- Never copy W-side entities, answers, titles, or sample-specific literals
  unless they independently occur in visible_c_inputs.
- Do not create unrelated corruption. Preserve enough useful C context for
  the result to remain a local neighbor.
- Damage one coherent behavior: weaken, remove, or blur an anchor, relation,
  qualifier, candidate distinction, evidence integration, output selection,
  or another matched failure pattern.
- An answer flip is not required and must not be claimed.
- Do not mention gold labels, oracle targets, evaluation metrics, source or
  target states, transport, prompt updates, or optimization in damaged_output.
- Do not wrap damaged_output in explanatory prose.
